workflow: RupeeKit Workflow 1 - Keyword Research and Content Plan

plan.searchIntent: Help employees estimate gratuity based on last drawn salary and completed years of service.
plan.targetPersona: Indian employees, HR professionals, individuals planning their finances and retirement benefits.
plan.primaryGoal: Provide an accurate and easy-to-use gratuity calculator for Indian employees, increasing site engagement and organic traffic.

plan.recommendedMetaTitle: Gratuity Calculator India: Estimate Your Gratuity Online
plan.recommendedMetaDescription: Use RupeeKit's Gratuity Calculator to estimate your gratuity in India. Get an accurate calculation based on your last drawn salary and years of
plan.h1: Gratuity Calculator India: Estimate Your Gratuity Online

inputs.name: lastDrawnBasicPlusDA
inputs.label: Last Drawn Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance (per month)
inputs.helpText: Enter your last drawn monthly Basic Salary plus Dearness Allowance.

inputs.name: totalYearsOfService
inputs.label: Total Years of Service (e.g., 5.75 for 5 years 9 months)
inputs.helpText: Enter your total service period in years, including months as decimals (e.g., 7 years 8 months is 7.67).

inputs.name: isCoveredUnderGratuityAct
inputs.label: Is your employer covered under the Gratuity Act, 1972? (1 for Yes, 0 for No)
inputs.helpText: Enter 1 if your employer is covered under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, otherwise enter 0.

inputs.name: effectiveYearsForCovered
inputs.label: Effective Years for Gratuity (if covered under Act)
inputs.helpText: If covered under the Act: Service of 5 years and 6 months or more counts as a full year (e.g., 5 years 7 months = 6 years, 5 years 5 months = 5 years). Enter this rounded value.

inputs.name: effectiveYearsForNonCovered
inputs.label: Effective Years for Gratuity (if NOT covered under Act)
inputs.helpText: If NOT covered under the Act: Usually based on completed years (e.g., 5 years 9 months = 5 years). Enter this truncated value.

calculatorSpec.formulaPlainEnglish: The gratuity calculation depends on whether the employer is covered under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972. If covered, the formula is (Last Drawn Basic + DA / 26) * 15 * Effective Years of Service (rounded up if months >= 6). If not covered, the common practice is (Last Drawn Basic + DA / 30) * 15 * Effective Years of Service (usually truncated). The calculator uses the 'isCoveredUnderGratuityAct' input to switch between these two methods.

contentBrief.introAngle: Understand the importance of gratuity as a valuable employee benefit in India. Our Gratuity Calculator helps you quickly estimate your potential payout.
mustCover: What is Gratuity and its significance in India?
Eligibility criteria for Gratuity under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 (5 years continuous service).
Detailed explanation of how Gratuity is calculated for employers covered under the Act.
Common practices for Gratuity calculation for employers not covered under the Act.
Key provisions of the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972.
Taxability of Gratuity in India for government and non-government employees.
Maximum Gratuity limit as per current regulations.
Factors affecting gratuity payment (resignation, termination, death, disability).
contentBrief.exampleScenario: An employee, Mr. Sharma, has completed 7 years and 8 months of service. His last drawn Basic Salary + Dearness Allowance is ₹50,000 per month. His employer is covered under the Gratuity Act, 1972. Calculate his estimated gratuity.
faqQuestions: Who is eligible for gratuity in India?
How is gratuity calculated if my employer is not covered under the Gratuity Act?
What is the maximum gratuity amount an employee can receive?
What is the difference between gratuity and provident fund (PF)?
trustNotes: Uses commonly followed Indian HRA exemption calculation rules for educational purposes.
Explains assumptions clearly so users can understand how the estimate is calculated.
Designed as an informational calculator, not as personalized tax or financial advice.
Users should verify final tax treatment with official guidance or a qualified tax professional.
contentBrief.disclaimer: This calculator is for educational and informational purposes only. It provides an estimate based on the inputs and assumptions shown. It is not tax, legal, investment, or financial advice. Please verify with official sources or consult a qualified professional before making tax or financial decisions.
